original_hypothesis.title: WildcatSanctionsSentinel overrideSanction/removeSanctionOverride are permissionless per-borrower key, potentially enabling sanctioned-escrow bypass if markets rely on msg.sender as borrower identity
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Sentinel lets any caller set sanctionOverrides[msg.sender][account]. If any market or integration mistakenly uses the wrong borrower key (e.g., msg.sender instead of the market's immutable borrower) when checking sanctions, an attacker could self-authorize overrides and bypass sanctions gating or escrow routing.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The current in-scope markets appear to correctly pass the market's immutable borrower into sentinel calls (_isSanctioned uses borrower immutably; borrow uses isFlaggedByChainalysis). However, this pattern is fragile and easy to misuse in future contracts or external integrations. A single incorrect borrower parameter would make overrides attacker-controlled.
deployment_assumptions: A market or other protocol contract/integration calls sentinel.isSanctioned with borrower parameter derived from msg.sender or other attacker-controlled value
That contract relies on sentinel overrides for access control or escrow routing
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: In this code, _isSanctioned uses the immutable borrower. No evidence of misuse in provided contracts; risk is architectural if extended.

proof_of_signal.pattern: sanctionOverrides[msg.sender][account] = true/false with no borrower authentication

preconditions: A consuming contract passes attacker-controlled borrower key into sentinel.isSanctioned
That check gates transfers/withdrawals/borrows or escrow release
Attacker can call overrideSanction(account) on sentinel prior to the gated action

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: permissionless writes to overrides keyed by msg.sender
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: any misuse of borrower parameter in consuming code makes overrides attacker-controlled
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No allowlist of legitimate borrowers enforced at sentinel layer

analysis_questions: Do any other (out-of-scope) protocol contracts call isSanctioned with borrower=msg.sender?
Do hooks contracts perform sanctions checks independently and might pass wrong borrower key?
Is there an intended constraint that only registered borrowers should be able to set overrides?
validation_requirements: Search entire repo (including hooks templates) for sentinel.isSanctioned usage
Add invariant tests ensuring borrower param always equals market.borrower
Evaluate whether sentinel should validate borrower is registered in ArchController

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If a future market mistakenly checks sentinel.isSanctioned(msg.sender, user), attacker calls overrideSanction(user) as msg.sender and bypasses sanctions gating to transfer/withdraw when otherwise blocked.
prerequisites: A downstream misuse bug exists outside this specific code path

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Potentially material if sanctions gating protects withdrawals/escrow routing
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires contract fixes/new deployments; sentinel overrides are mutable though

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Likely missing cross-contract invariant tests on borrower parameter consistency

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Monitor unusual overrides from non-borrower EOAs

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
